uses: Relief of mild to moderate pain including headache, toothache, backache, menstrual pain. Reduction of fever.
dosage_pregnancy: Safe in all trimesters at recommended doses
side_effects: Rare at normal doses: nausea, allergic reactions; overdose causes liver damage
warnings: Do not exceed 4g/day; avoid alcohol; caution in liver disease; check other products for paracetamol
contraindications: Severe hepatic impairment, hypersensitivity to paracetamol

uses: Fever and mild to moderate pain relief, especially in children. Used for teething pain, immunization fever.
side_effects: Rare: skin rash, nausea; overdose causes hepatotoxicity
warnings: Avoid in severe liver disease; do not double dose; check for paracetamol in other medicines
contraindications: Hepatic impairment, paracetamol hypersensitivity

uses: Relief of mild to moderate pain, inflammation, and fever. Used for arthritis, dysmenorrhea, dental pain, sports injuries.
dosage_adult: 200-400mg every 4-6 hours; max 1200mg/day OTC or 3200mg/day prescribed
dosage_elderly: Start with lowest dose; monitor renal function
dosage_pregnancy: Avoid after 20 weeks; safe in 1st trimester with caution
side_effects: GI upset, nausea, stomach pain, dizziness, edema, elevated blood pressure
warnings: Take with food; avoid in peptic ulcer, renal impairment; avoid in 3rd trimester of pregnancy; increases cardiovascular risk
contraindications: Peptic ulcer disease, severe renal/hepatic impairment, NSAID allergy, 3rd trimester pregnancy
drug_interactions: Aspirin, warfarin, ACE inhibitors, lithium, methotrexate

uses: Pain relief, fever reduction, anti-inflammatory. Low dose used for prevention of heart attack and stroke. Headache, cold, flu.
dosage_child_2_12: NOT recommended due to Reye's syndrome risk
dosage_elderly: 75-150mg for cardiovascular protection; monitor for GI bleeding
dosage_pregnancy: Avoid especially in 3rd trimester; low dose may be used under supervision
side_effects: GI bleeding, stomach irritation, nausea, tinnitus (ringing in ears), bruising
warnings: Never give to children under 16; avoid in bleeding disorders; take with food; avoid with anticoagulants
contraindications: Children under 16, bleeding disorders, active peptic ulcer, severe renal failure

symptoms_treated: menstrual pain,dental pain,headache,mild to moderate pain,inflammatory pain
uses: Relief of mild to moderate pain including dysmenorrhea (menstrual cramps), dental pain, headache, muscular pain. Short-term use only.
dosage_adult: 500mg initially then 250mg every 6 hours; max 7 days
dosage_elderly: Use with caution; renal monitoring required
side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, stomach pain, dizziness, headache, skin rash
warnings: Short-term use only (max 7 days); avoid in kidney disease; take with food; stop if diarrhea occurs
contraindications: Renal impairment, inflammatory bowel disease, peptic ulcer, pregnancy (3rd trimester)

uses: Rheumatoid arthritis, osteoarthritis, ankylosing spondylitis, acute gout, back pain, postoperative pain, dysmenorrhea.
dosage_adult: 50mg 2-3 times/day or 75mg SR twice daily; max 150mg/day
dosage_elderly: Use lowest effective dose; monitor renal function
dosage_pregnancy: Avoid; use cautiously in 1st and 2nd trimester only
side_effects: GI upset, nausea, headache, dizziness, edema, elevated liver enzymes
warnings: Take with food; avoid in peptic ulcer; cardiovascular risk with prolonged use; monitor kidney function
contraindications: Peptic ulcer, severe hepatic/renal failure, heart failure, 3rd trimester pregnancy

symptoms_treated: muscle pain,back pain,spasm,neck pain,musculoskeletal pain,tension headache
uses: Musculoskeletal pain with muscle spasm, back pain, neck pain, tension headache, fibromyalgia-related pain.
dosage_elderly: Use with caution; lower doses preferred
side_effects: Drowsiness, dry mouth, blurred vision, urinary retention, confusion
warnings: Avoid driving/machinery; avoid alcohol; caution in elderly; not for children
contraindications: Glaucoma, urinary retention, myasthenia gravis, children under 12

uses: Acute pain, dysmenorrhea, dental pain, arthritis, sports injuries. Faster onset than diclofenac sodium.
side_effects: GI discomfort, nausea, headache, dizziness, elevated liver enzymes
warnings: Avoid in peptic ulcer; take with food; cardiovascular risk
contraindications: Active peptic ulcer, severe renal/hepatic impairment, 3rd trimester pregnancy

uses: Cold and flu symptoms including nasal congestion, sinus pain, headache, body aches, and fever.
side_effects: Insomnia, nervousness, increased blood pressure, GI upset, dizziness
warnings: Avoid in hypertension, heart disease, glaucoma; do not take with MAOIs; may cause insomnia
contraindications: Hypertension, coronary artery disease, glaucoma, MAOI use, hyperthyroidism

uses: Symptomatic relief of nasal congestion, rhinitis, sinusitis, common cold, allergic rhinitis.
dosage_elderly: Lower doses; monitor for CNS and cardiac effects
side_effects: Drowsiness, dry mouth, blurred vision, urinary retention, insomnia
warnings: May cause drowsiness; avoid alcohol; avoid in hypertension; do not drive; avoid in glaucoma
contraindications: Hypertension, coronary artery disease, glaucoma, MAOI use

uses: Seasonal and perennial allergic rhinitis, chronic urticaria (hives), allergic conjunctivitis, itching.
dosage_child_under_2: 2.5mg once daily (1-2 years only under supervision)
side_effects: Mild drowsiness, dry mouth, headache, fatigue
warnings: May cause mild sedation; use with caution while driving; reduce dose in renal impairment
contraindications: Severe renal impairment (CrCl <10ml/min), hypersensitivity

uses: Allergic rhinitis, chronic urticaria, allergic skin conditions, hay fever, itching.
side_effects: Mild drowsiness, dry mouth, headache, fatigue
warnings: Mild sedating; reduce dose in renal impairment; avoid with alcohol
contraindications: Severe renal failure, hypersensitivity to cetirizine or hydroxyzine

uses: Seasonal allergic rhinitis, chronic idiopathic urticaria (hives), hay fever. Non-sedating.
dosage_elderly: Standard dose; caution in renal impairment
side_effects: Headache, nausea, dizziness, drowsiness (rare)
warnings: Avoid with fruit juices (reduces absorption); take with water; reduce dose in renal impairment
contraindications: Severe renal impairment, children under 6

uses: Seasonal and perennial allergic rhinitis, chronic urticaria. Non-sedating antihistamine.
side_effects: Headache, dry mouth, fatigue, nausea (rare)
warnings: Minimal sedation; reduce dose in hepatic/renal impairment; take on empty stomach for best absorption
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to loratadine, children under 2

uses: Allergic rhinitis, urticaria, hay fever. Similar to Claritin.
side_effects: Headache, dry mouth, drowsiness (rare)
warnings: Reduce dose in hepatic impairment
contraindications: Hypersensitivity, children under 2

uses: Treatment of bacterial infections including sinusitis, tonsillitis, otitis media, UTIs, lower respiratory tract infections, skin and soft tissue infections.
dosage_adult: 625mg (500/125) every 8 hours or 1g (875/125) every 12 hours; 7-10 days
dosage_child_2_12: 25-45mg/kg/day of amoxicillin component in divided doses
dosage_elderly: Standard dose; renal adjustment if CrCl <30ml/min
side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, vomiting, skin rash, candidiasis
warnings: Ensure full course completed; take with food to reduce GI side effects; caution in penicillin allergy
contraindications: Penicillin/amoxicillin allergy, history of jaundice with amoxicillin-clavulanate

uses: Bacterial infections resistant to amoxicillin alone. Similar indications to Augmentin.
side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, skin rash, candidal overgrowth
warnings: Complete the full course; take with food
contraindications: Penicillin allergy, previous cholestatic jaundice with amoxiclav

uses: Community-acquired pneumonia, sinusitis, tonsillitis, pharyngitis, otitis media, skin infections, STIs including chlamydia, typhoid.
dosage_adult: 500mg once daily for 3-5 days; typhoid: 1g/day for 5 days
dosage_elderly: Standard dose; caution in hepatic impairment
side_effects: Nausea, diarrhea, abdominal pain, QT prolongation (rare), hearing changes
warnings: Avoid in QT prolongation; complete full course; take 1 hour before or 2 hours after meals
contraindications: QT prolongation, severe hepatic impairment, hypersensitivity to macrolides
drug_interactions: Antacids (reduce absorption), warfarin, digoxin, QT-prolonging drugs

uses: UTIs, typhoid fever, bacterial diarrhea, bone and joint infections, prostatitis, respiratory tract infections.
dosage_adult: 250-750mg every 12 hours; typhoid: 500mg twice daily for 10-14 days
dosage_child_2_12: Not recommended (affects cartilage); used only for specific severe infections
dosage_elderly: Renal dose adjustment; tendon rupture risk
side_effects: Nausea, diarrhea, dizziness, photosensitivity, tendon rupture, QT prolongation
warnings: Avoid in children/adolescents; tendon rupture risk (especially in elderly); avoid prolonged sun exposure; take with plenty of water
contraindications: Children under 18 (unless severe), pregnancy, history of tendon disorders with fluoroquinolones

uses: Protozoal infections (amoebiasis, giardiasis, trichomoniasis), anaerobic bacterial infections, H. pylori (with other drugs), dental infections, bacterial vaginosis.
dosage_adult: 400-500mg 3 times/day for 5-10 days; amoebiasis: 800mg 3 times/day for 5 days
dosage_pregnancy: Avoid in 1st trimester; safe in 2nd and 3rd with caution
side_effects: Nausea, metallic taste, dark urine, headache, dizziness, peripheral neuropathy (long use)
warnings: Avoid alcohol (severe reaction); complete course; avoid in 1st trimester
contraindications: 1st trimester pregnancy, alcohol use, hypersensitivity

uses: Urinary tract infections, otitis media, pharyngitis, tonsillitis, lower respiratory infections, typhoid, uncomplicated gonorrhea.
side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, abdominal pain, skin rash, headache
warnings: Penicillin cross-sensitivity; complete full course; take with food if GI upset
contraindications: Cephalosporin allergy, severe renal impairment

uses: Skin and soft tissue infections, respiratory infections, UTIs, otitis media, osteomyelitis.
side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, skin rash, pseudomembranous colitis (rare)
warnings: Penicillin cross-sensitivity; complete full course
contraindications: Cephalosporin allergy

uses: Community-acquired pneumonia, sinusitis, pharyngitis, H. pylori eradication (triple therapy), skin infections, MAC infection in HIV.
side_effects: Nausea, diarrhea, abdominal pain, metallic taste, QT prolongation
warnings: Avoid in pregnancy; caution with QT-prolonging drugs; monitor liver function
contraindications: Pregnancy, QT prolongation, severe hepatic impairment
drug_interactions: Statins (myopathy), warfarin, carbamazepine, QT-prolonging drugs, digoxin

uses: Same as Klaricid. Respiratory infections, H. pylori eradication, skin infections.
side_effects: GI disturbances, metallic taste, headache, QT prolongation
warnings: Avoid in pregnancy; QT prolongation risk
contraindications: Pregnancy, QT prolongation, severe hepatic failure

uses: Severe bacterial infections: meningitis, pneumonia, septicemia, intra-abdominal infections, typhoid, gonorrhea, Lyme disease.
dosage_elderly: Standard dose; renal adjustment if very severe impairment
side_effects: Local injection site reactions, diarrhea, skin rash, pseudomembranous colitis, biliary sludge
warnings: Do not mix with calcium-containing IV solutions; avoid in neonates with hyperbilirubinemia; hospital use
contraindications: Cephalosporin allergy, neonates with hyperbilirubinemia, with calcium-containing IV fluids

uses: Respiratory tract infections, sinusitis, otitis media, UTIs, skin infections, Lyme disease.
side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, skin rash, headache
warnings: Complete the course; take with food for better absorption of suspension
contraindications: Cephalosporin allergy

uses: Same as Cefspan. Urinary tract infections, otitis media, typhoid, uncomplicated gonorrhea.
side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, rash
warnings: Complete course; penicillin cross-allergy possible
contraindications: Cephalosporin allergy

uses: Skin and soft tissue infections, UTIs, respiratory infections, bone infections, otitis media.
side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, skin rash
warnings: Complete the course; penicillin cross-allergy
contraindications: Cephalosporin allergy

uses: Identical indications to Azomax. Respiratory infections, chlamydia, typhoid, skin infections.
dosage_elderly: Standard dose; caution in hepatic disease
side_effects: Nausea, diarrhea, abdominal pain, QT prolongation
warnings: QT prolongation risk; take 1 hour before meals
contraindications: QT prolongation, macrolide hypersensitivity

uses: Tonsillitis, otitis media, sinusitis, UTIs, community-acquired pneumonia, H. pylori (with other agents), skin infections.
side_effects: Diarrhea, nausea, skin rash (especially in viral infections), candidal overgrowth
warnings: Complete the course; avoid in penicillin allergy; rash common in viral infections (not allergy)
contraindications: Penicillin allergy, infectious mononucleosis

uses: GERD, peptic ulcer disease, Zollinger-Ellison syndrome, H. pylori eradication (part of triple therapy), prevention of NSAID-induced ulcers.
dosage_adult: 20-40mg once daily before breakfast; 4-8 weeks for ulcers
dosage_pregnancy: Category C; use if benefits outweigh risks
side_effects: Headache, nausea, diarrhea, abdominal pain, hypomagnesemia (long term), C. diff risk
warnings: Long-term use risks: fractures, hypomagnesemia, C. difficile; take 30-60 mins before meals
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to PPIs
drug_interactions: Clopidogrel (reduced effect), methotrexate, digoxin, warfarin, ketoconazole

uses: Acid reflux, heartburn, GERD, peptic ulcer, gastritis. Generic alternative to Risek.
side_effects: Headache, nausea, diarrhea, flatulence
warnings: Hypomagnesemia with long-term use; fracture risk
contraindications: PPI hypersensitivity

uses: GERD, erosive esophagitis, H. pylori eradication, Zollinger-Ellison syndrome, NSAID-induced ulcer prevention.
side_effects: Headache, diarrhea, nausea, flatulence, abdominal pain
warnings: Long-term risks as with all PPIs; take before meals
contraindications: PPI hypersensitivity, coadministration with rilpivirine

uses: GERD, erosive esophagitis, peptic ulcer, Zollinger-Ellison syndrome. Fewer drug interactions than omeprazole.
side_effects: Headache, diarrhea, nausea, abdominal pain, flatulence
warnings: Fewer drug interactions than omeprazole; long-term use risks apply
contraindications: PPI hypersensitivity

uses: GERD, peptic ulcer, erosive esophagitis. Brand of pantoprazole.
side_effects: Headache, diarrhea, nausea, flatulence
warnings: Same as Zurcal
contraindications: PPI hypersensitivity

generic_name: Sodium Alginate + Sodium Bicarbonate + Calcium Carbonate
uses: Symptomatic relief of heartburn and acid reflux, particularly after meals or at night. Forms a raft on stomach contents.
dosage_adult: 10-20ml or 2-4 tablets after meals and at bedtime
side_effects: Rarely: constipation, diarrhea, sodium overload in susceptible patients
warnings: High sodium content — caution in heart failure and hypertension; not a substitute for PPI in severe GERD
contraindications: Severe renal impairment, low sodium diet
drug_interactions: May reduce absorption of other drugs if taken together

uses: Rapid relief of heartburn, indigestion, and acid stomach. Chewable antacid.
dosage_adult: 2 tablets after meals and at bedtime (max 16 tablets/day)
dosage_child_2_12: 1 tablet; not recommended under 6 without advice
side_effects: Constipation (calcium), diarrhea (magnesium), rebound acidity
warnings: Do not use as sole therapy for GERD; excessive use causes electrolyte imbalance
contraindications: Hypercalcemia, severe renal impairment
drug_interactions: Reduces absorption of tetracyclines, fluoroquinolones, iron

uses: Nausea, vomiting, bloating, gastroparesis, gastro-oesophageal reflux in children.
dosage_child_under_2: Suspension 0.25mg/kg 3 times/day; highest risk of cardiac effects
side_effects: QT prolongation (rare), dry mouth, galactorrhea, headache
warnings: Maximum 1 week use; cardiac risk at high doses or with QT-prolonging drugs; avoid in children under 35kg
contraindications: QT prolongation, hepatic failure, prolactin-secreting tumors

uses: Nausea, vomiting, bloating. Domperidone brand in Pakistan.
side_effects: QT prolongation, dry mouth, galactorrhea
warnings: Short-term use; cardiac monitoring
contraindications: QT prolongation, hepatic failure

uses: Traveler's diarrhea, indigestion, nausea, stomach upset, part of H. pylori eradication.
dosage_adult: 30ml or 2 tablets every 30-60 min; max 8 doses/day
side_effects: Black tongue/stool, tinnitus (high doses), constipation
warnings: Contains salicylate — avoid in aspirin allergy and children with viral illness (Reye's risk)
contraindications: Aspirin allergy, children under 12 with viral illness, pregnancy

uses: Acute asthma attacks, bronchospasm, COPD, exercise-induced bronchospasm. Rescue inhaler.
dosage_adult: Inhaler: 100-200mcg (1-2 puffs) every 4-6 hours as needed; Nebulizer: 2.5mg in 2.5ml saline
dosage_child_2_12: 100mcg (1 puff) every 4-6 hours as needed; Nebulizer: 1.25-2.5mg
dosage_elderly: Standard inhaled dose; caution in cardiac disease
dosage_pregnancy: Category C; safe when necessary for asthma
side_effects: Tremor, palpitations, tachycardia, headache, hypokalemia (high doses)
warnings: Rescue inhaler only; if using >2 times/week, controller therapy needed; overuse masks worsening asthma
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to salbutamol
available_forms: MDI Inhaler 100mcg/dose; Nebulizing solution; Tablet 2mg, 4mg; Syrup

uses: Maintenance therapy for asthma and COPD. Combination of inhaled steroid and long-acting bronchodilator.
dosage_adult: 1 puff (50/100 or 50/250 or 50/500 mcg) twice daily
dosage_pregnancy: Use if benefits outweigh risks; continue for asthma control
side_effects: Oral candidiasis, hoarseness, throat irritation; systemic effects with high doses
warnings: Not a rescue inhaler; rinse mouth after use to prevent oral thrush; do not stop suddenly
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to components; not for acute attacks

uses: Maintenance therapy for asthma and COPD. Also approved for SMART (maintenance and reliever therapy).
dosage_adult: 1-2 puffs (160/4.5 or 320/9 mcg) 1-2 times daily; SMART: 1-2 puffs as needed
dosage_pregnancy: Safer than uncontrolled asthma; continue if needed
side_effects: Oral candidiasis, hoarseness, palpitations, tremor
warnings: Rinse mouth after use; not for acute attack (unless SMART approach); gradual dose reduction only
contraindications: Hypersensitivity; not for sole acute bronchospasm treatment

uses: Maintenance therapy for asthma. Inhaled corticosteroid for airway inflammation control.
side_effects: Oral candidiasis, hoarseness, adrenal suppression (high doses)
warnings: Rinse mouth after use; do not stop suddenly; not a rescue inhaler
contraindications: Hypersensitivity; status asthmaticus alone

uses: COPD maintenance, acute asthma in combination with salbutamol, chronic bronchitis, emphysema.
dosage_elderly: Standard dose; caution in glaucoma and urinary retention
side_effects: Dry mouth, urinary retention, constipation, blurred vision, palpitations
warnings: Avoid in glaucoma; avoid eye contact with nebulized solution; not first-choice for asthma
contraindications: Allergy to atropine, glaucoma (open-angle fine with care)

uses: Prophylaxis and chronic treatment of asthma, allergic rhinitis, exercise-induced bronchoconstriction.
dosage_child_2_12: 5mg chewable tablet once daily (6-14 years); 4mg granules or chewable (2-5 years)
side_effects: Headache, abdominal pain, neuropsychiatric effects (mood changes, depression, suicidality – rare)
warnings: Monitor for mood/behavior changes; not for acute attacks; contains phenylalanine in chewable tablets
contraindications: Phenylketonuria (chewable), hypersensitivity

uses: Acute relief of bronchospasm in asthma and COPD. Rescue inhaler — similar to Ventolin.
side_effects: Tremor, palpitations, tachycardia, headache
warnings: Rescue use only; overuse indicates poor control
contraindications: Salbutamol hypersensitivity

uses: First-line treatment for type 2 diabetes mellitus. Also used for prediabetes and PCOS.
dosage_adult: 500mg twice or thrice daily with meals; max 2550mg/day
dosage_elderly: Start with 500mg; monitor renal function; avoid if eGFR <30
dosage_pregnancy: Category B; often used in gestational diabetes and PCOS
side_effects: Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, metallic taste, lactic acidosis (rare)
warnings: Hold before contrast media procedures; avoid in renal impairment (eGFR <30); monitor B12 levels long-term
contraindications: eGFR <30ml/min, hepatic failure, alcohol abuse, contrast media procedures

uses: Type 2 diabetes mellitus. Stimulates insulin secretion from pancreatic beta cells.
dosage_adult: 1-4mg once daily with first main meal; max 8mg/day
side_effects: Hypoglycemia, weight gain, nausea, dizziness, skin reactions
warnings: Risk of hypoglycemia especially with missed meals; avoid alcohol; monitor blood glucose
contraindications: Type 1 diabetes, DKA, renal/hepatic failure, pregnancy
drug_interactions: Alcohol, NSAIDs, salicylates, warfarin, MAOIs, beta-blockers

uses: Type 2 diabetes. Second-generation sulfonylurea with additional antiplatelet effects.
dosage_elderly: Start low; MR preferred; hypoglycemia risk
side_effects: Hypoglycemia, weight gain, GI upset
warnings: Hypoglycemia risk with missed meals; alcohol increases risk
contraindications: Type 1 diabetes, DKA, pregnancy

uses: Type 2 diabetes as monotherapy or combination. Inhibits DPP-4 enzyme, increases incretin levels.
dosage_elderly: Dose based on renal function; no dose reduction normally needed
side_effects: Nasopharyngitis, headache, pancreatitis (rare), joint pain
warnings: Risk of pancreatitis; renal dose adjustment; discontinue if pancreatitis suspected
contraindications: Type 1 diabetes, DKA, pancreatitis history

uses: Type 2 diabetes, as monotherapy or combination with metformin, sulfonylureas, or insulin.
dosage_adult: 50mg twice daily (monotherapy or with insulin/sulfonylurea: once daily)
dosage_elderly: Standard dose; caution in renal impairment
side_effects: Nasopharyngitis, dizziness, headache, peripheral edema, liver enzyme elevation
warnings: Monitor liver function; pancreatitis risk; renal dose adjustment
contraindications: Severe renal impairment, hepatic impairment

uses: Type 2 diabetes when both sitagliptin and metformin are appropriate.
dosage_elderly: Renal dose adjustment; avoid if eGFR <45
side_effects: Nausea, diarrhea, nasopharyngitis, headache, pancreatitis (rare)
warnings: Hold before contrast; lactic acidosis risk from metformin component
contraindications: eGFR <45, hepatic failure, pancreatitis history

uses: Type 2 diabetes. Brand of glimepiride by Sanofi.
side_effects: Hypoglycemia, weight gain, nausea
warnings: Hypoglycemia risk; avoid in renal/hepatic failure
contraindications: Type 1 diabetes, DKA, pregnancy

uses: Type 2 diabetes, PCOS. Metformin brand alternative to Glucophage.
dosage_pregnancy: Safe in PCOS and gestational diabetes under supervision
side_effects: GI upset, metallic taste, lactic acidosis (rare)
warnings: Avoid with contrast media; monitor renal function
contraindications: eGFR <30, hepatic failure

uses: Hypertension, stable angina, vasospastic angina, coronary artery disease.
side_effects: Peripheral edema (ankle swelling), headache, flushing, dizziness, palpitations
warnings: Ankle edema common; hepatic dose adjustment; avoid abrupt withdrawal in angina
contraindications: Cardiogenic shock, severe aortic stenosis, unstable angina (without beta-blocker)

uses: Hypertension, stable chronic heart failure, angina. Cardioselective beta-blocker.
side_effects: Bradycardia, fatigue, cold extremities, dizziness, bronchospasm (less than non-selective)
warnings: Do not stop abruptly (rebound angina); mask hypoglycemia symptoms; caution in asthma
contraindications: Severe asthma, AV block, cardiogenic shock, severe bradycardia

uses: Hypertension, angina, post-myocardial infarction, arrhythmias. Long-standing beta-blocker.
side_effects: Bradycardia, fatigue, cold extremities, dizziness, bronchospasm
warnings: Do not stop abruptly; renal dose adjustment; caution in asthma and diabetes
contraindications: Asthma, AV block, severe bradycardia, cardiogenic shock

uses: Hypertension, diabetic nephropathy (type 2 diabetes), heart failure, stroke prevention in hypertension with LVH.
dosage_elderly: Start 25-50mg; no specific renal adjustment needed
side_effects: Dizziness, hyperkalemia, renal impairment, upper respiratory infection
warnings: Avoid in pregnancy; monitor potassium and renal function; avoid with ACE inhibitor combination
contraindications: Pregnancy, bilateral renal artery stenosis, hyperkalemia
drug_interactions: NSAIDs, potassium supplements, ACE inhibitors, rifampicin

uses: Hypertension, cardiovascular risk reduction in high-risk patients. Longest-acting ARB.
side_effects: Dizziness, upper respiratory tract infection, diarrhea, hyperkalemia
warnings: Avoid in pregnancy; hepatic dose adjustment; monitor potassium
contraindications: Pregnancy, severe hepatic impairment, biliary obstruction

uses: Hypertension not controlled by single agent. Combination of CCB and ARB for additive blood pressure reduction.
side_effects: Peripheral edema, dizziness, headache, hyperkalemia, flushing
warnings: Avoid in pregnancy; do not combine with ACE inhibitor + ARB; monitor electrolytes
contraindications: Pregnancy, bilateral renal artery stenosis, diabetic patients on aliskiren
drug_interactions: NSAIDs, potassium supplements, ACE inhibitors, cyclosporine

symptoms_treated: hypertension,benign prostatic hyperplasia,high blood pressure,urinary retention
uses: Hypertension, benign prostatic hyperplasia (BPH) — improves urinary flow.
side_effects: First-dose hypotension, dizziness, fatigue, edema, retrograde ejaculation
warnings: First-dose effect (take at bedtime); avoid in orthostatic hypotension; intraoperative floppy iris syndrome
contraindications: Hypersensitivity, hepatic impairment (extended release)
drug_interactions: Other antihypertensives, PDE-5 inhibitors (severe hypotension), diuretics

symptoms_treated: hypertension,heart failure,post-MI cardioprotection,diabetic nephropathy,high blood pressure
uses: Hypertension, heart failure, post-MI cardioprotection, diabetic nephropathy, high cardiovascular risk reduction.
side_effects: Dry cough, dizziness, hyperkalemia, angioedema (rare), renal impairment
warnings: Avoid in pregnancy; persistent dry cough common; stop if angioedema; monitor K+ and creatinine
contraindications: Pregnancy, history of angioedema with ACE inhibitor, bilateral renal artery stenosis
drug_interactions: NSAIDs, potassium supplements, ARBs, aliskiren, lithium

symptoms_treated: heart attack prevention,stroke prevention,antiplatelet,clot prevention,cardiovascular disease
uses: Prevention of myocardial infarction and stroke. Antiplatelet therapy in cardiovascular disease.
dosage_child_2_12: Not used for antiplatelet purposes; Kawasaki disease only under specialist supervision
dosage_elderly: 75mg once daily; monitor for GI bleeding
dosage_pregnancy: Low-dose may be used in pre-eclampsia under supervision; avoid in 3rd trimester
side_effects: GI bleeding, stomach irritation, bruising, tinnitus (overdose)
warnings: Take with food or after meals; avoid with other NSAIDs; stop 7-10 days before surgery
contraindications: Active peptic ulcer, bleeding disorders, children under 16 for analgesic use

uses: Primary hypercholesterolemia, mixed dyslipidemia, cardiovascular event prevention, familial hypercholesterolemia.
dosage_child_2_12: 10-20mg once daily (10-17 years with familial hypercholesterolemia)
side_effects: Myopathy/myalgia, rhabdomyolysis (rare), elevated liver enzymes, headache, GI upset
warnings: Contraindicated in pregnancy; monitor liver function; avoid with certain drugs that increase levels
contraindications: Active liver disease, pregnancy, breastfeeding

uses: Hypercholesterolemia, primary prevention of cardiovascular events, mixed dyslipidemia.
side_effects: Myalgia, myopathy, headache, constipation, abdominal pain, proteinuria
warnings: Contraindicated in pregnancy; monitor for muscle symptoms; renal monitoring
contraindications: Active liver disease, pregnancy, severe renal impairment, myopathy

uses: Hypercholesterolemia, cardiovascular event prevention, type 2 diabetic dyslipidemia.
dosage_adult: 10-40mg once daily in the evening; max 80mg (restricted)
side_effects: Myopathy (dose-dependent), elevated liver enzymes, headache, GI upset
warnings: 80mg dose avoided due to myopathy risk; drug interactions limit dose; contraindicated in pregnancy
contraindications: Active liver disease, pregnancy, concomitant strong CYP3A4 inhibitors
drug_interactions: Amlodipine (max 20mg simvastatin), amiodarone, cyclosporine, many antibiotics

generic_name: Multivitamin + Zinc (B-complex with Vitamin C and Zinc)
symptoms_treated: vitamin deficiency,zinc deficiency,fatigue,weakness,poor wound healing,immune support
uses: Prevention and treatment of vitamin B-complex and zinc deficiencies, general nutrition support, post-illness recovery.
dosage_pregnancy: Use as directed by physician; folic acid may be insufficient for pregnancy needs
side_effects: Nausea (with zinc), yellow discoloration of urine (riboflavin), GI upset
warnings: May cause yellow urine (normal); take after food to reduce GI discomfort; zinc can interfere with copper absorption long-term
contraindications: Hypervitaminosis
drug_interactions: Zinc reduces absorption of quinolones, tetracyclines

uses: Peripheral neuropathy, diabetic neuropathy, vitamin B1/B6/B12 deficiency, nerve regeneration support.
dosage_adult: 1 tablet 1-3 times daily or 1 ampoule IM/IV injection
side_effects: Peripheral neuropathy from excess B6 (high doses), injection site pain, allergic reactions
warnings: Excess B6 (>200mg/day) causes sensory neuropathy; injection form for severe deficiency
contraindications: Hypersensitivity; avoid high-dose B6 long-term
drug_interactions: Levodopa (B6 reduces efficacy unless combined with carbidopa), isoniazid

uses: Peripheral neuropathy, vitamin B12 deficiency, megaloblastic anemia, diabetic neuropathy.
side_effects: Rare: mild GI discomfort, injection site pain
warnings: Different from cyanocobalamin; better absorbed; preferred in neuropathy
contraindications: Hypersensitivity to cobalamin
drug_interactions: Metformin, PPIs, H2-blockers reduce B12 absorption

symptoms_treated: calcium deficiency,bone weakness,osteoporosis,vitamin C deficiency,immune weakness,muscle cramps
uses: Calcium and Vitamin C deficiency, osteoporosis prevention, immune support, muscle cramp prevention.
dosage_adult: 1 effervescent tablet dissolved in water once or twice daily
side_effects: GI discomfort, constipation, nausea
warnings: Hypercalcemia risk with excessive intake; avoid with tetracyclines
contraindications: Hypercalcemia, kidney stones
drug_interactions: Tetracyclines, fluoroquinolones, iron (reduces absorption if taken together)

uses: General nutritional supplementation, prevention of vitamin and mineral deficiencies, immune support.
side_effects: Nausea (if taken on empty stomach), yellow urine (B vitamins), constipation (iron)
warnings: Take with food; iron-containing formulas can cause constipation; avoid in hemochromatosis
contraindications: Hemochromatosis, hypervitaminosis A or D
drug_interactions: Iron reduces absorption of quinolones, tetracyclines

symptoms_treated: osteoporosis,calcium deficiency,vitamin D deficiency,bone pain,fracture risk
uses: Osteoporosis prevention and treatment, calcium and vitamin D deficiency, post-menopausal bone health.
dosage_elderly: 1 tablet twice daily; essential for osteoporosis
side_effects: Constipation, flatulence, hypercalcemia (excess), kidney stones
warnings: Take with meals; avoid in hypercalcemia; limit dose in renal impairment
contraindications: Hypercalcemia, kidney stones, severe renal impairment

uses: Vitamin E deficiency, antioxidant support, skin health, male and female fertility support, intermittent claudication.
dosage_adult: 400 IU once daily; deficiency: up to 1000 IU/day
dosage_pregnancy: Safe in normal doses (400 IU); avoid megadoses
side_effects: Nausea, diarrhea, bleeding (very high doses), fatigue
warnings: Megadoses increase bleeding risk; avoid with anticoagulants at high doses
contraindications: Hypervitaminosis E, active bleeding
drug_interactions: Warfarin (increased bleeding at high doses), vitamin K

uses: Comprehensive nutritional supplement for prevention of multiple vitamin and mineral deficiencies.
side_effects: Nausea, yellow urine, GI discomfort, constipation
warnings: Take with food; avoid in hemochromatosis; not a replacement for specific deficiency treatment
contraindications: Hemochromatosis, hypervitaminosis

symptoms_treated: vitamin D deficiency,rickets,osteomalacia,bone pain,muscle weakness,immune weakness,fatigue
uses: Vitamin D deficiency, rickets in children, osteomalacia, osteoporosis prevention, immune modulation.
dosage_adult: 1000-2000 IU daily (maintenance); 50,000 IU weekly for 8 weeks (deficiency treatment)
dosage_child_2_12: 400-1000 IU daily (maintenance); treat deficiency per protocol
dosage_child_under_2: 400 IU daily (prevention); consult physician for deficiency
dosage_elderly: 1000-2000 IU daily; higher if serum 25-OHD very low
side_effects: Hypercalcemia (excess), constipation, nausea, kidney stones
warnings: Monitor serum 25-OHD levels; excessive supplementation causes toxicity; dissolve in water/milk
contraindications: Hypercalcemia, hypervitaminosis D, sarcoidosis

uses: Prevention and treatment of bacterial skin infections, infected wounds, cuts, burns, abrasions.
dosage_adult: Apply thin layer to affected area 1-4 times daily
dosage_child_2_12: Apply thin layer to affected area 2-3 times daily
side_effects: Contact dermatitis, local skin irritation, allergic reaction
warnings: For external use only; avoid in large wounds or prolonged use (systemic absorption risk with polymyxin B); avoid in ears if perforated eardrum
contraindications: Known allergy to polymyxin or bacitracin

uses: Staphylococcal skin infections, impetigo, infected eczema, folliculitis, infected wounds.
dosage_adult: Apply to affected area 3-4 times daily for up to 10-14 days
side_effects: Local irritation, contact dermatitis, burning sensation
warnings: Avoid prolonged use to prevent resistance; avoid near eyes; for external use only
contraindications: Fusidic acid hypersensitivity

uses: Inflammatory and pruritic skin conditions including eczema, psoriasis, contact dermatitis, seborrheic dermatitis.
dosage_adult: Apply thin layer 1-2 times daily; max 2 weeks on face
dosage_child_2_12: Apply sparingly once daily; limit to 5-7 days on face
dosage_child_under_2: Not recommended except under specialist supervision
dosage_pregnancy: Avoid extensive use; limited to small area short-term
side_effects: Skin thinning, striae, telangiectasia, acne, hypertrichosis (prolonged use), systemic effects with extensive use
warnings: Do not use on face for long term; avoid on infected skin; taper dose; not for nappy area in children
contraindications: Rosacea, acne, perioral dermatitis, fungal infections, viral skin infections

uses: Acne vulgaris, rosacea, post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation, mild to moderate inflammatory acne.
dosage_adult: Apply thin layer to affected area twice daily (morning and evening)
dosage_pregnancy: Category B; safe in 2nd and 3rd trimester; consult physician
side_effects: Tingling, burning, stinging on application, skin dryness, itching
warnings: Avoid eyes and mouth; use sunscreen; may cause temporary lightening of dark skin
contraindications: Azelaic acid hypersensitivity

symptoms_treated: infected eczema,mixed skin infection,skin inflammation with bacterial or fungal infection
uses: Inflammatory skin conditions with bacterial or fungal secondary infection, infected eczema, intertrigo.
dosage_child_under_2: Avoid or use under strict physician supervision
side_effects: Skin thinning (steroid), sensitization to neomycin, systemic absorption with extensive use
warnings: Avoid prolonged use; avoid on face; neomycin sensitization risk; not for viral or primary fungal infections alone
contraindications: Tuberculosis of skin, herpes simplex, varicella, neomycin hypersensitivity

symptoms_treated: mixed skin infection,tinea,fungal infection with inflammation,infected eczema
uses: Inflammatory dermatoses with concurrent bacterial and fungal infection, tinea infections with secondary inflammation.
dosage_adult: Apply thin layer to affected area 2-3 times daily
dosage_child_2_12: Apply sparingly once or twice daily
dosage_child_under_2: Avoid or use under strict supervision
dosage_elderly: Limit duration; monitor for systemic effects
side_effects: Skin thinning, acne, striae, gentamicin sensitization, skin discoloration (clioquinol)
warnings: Short-term use only; avoid on face; clioquinol may stain clothing and skin
contraindications: Viral skin diseases, tuberculosis of skin, known hypersensitivity to components

uses: Generalized anxiety disorder (GAD), panic disorder, social anxiety disorder. Short-term treatment only.
dosage_adult: 0.25-0.5mg 3 times/day; panic disorder: up to 10mg/day in divided doses
dosage_elderly: 0.25mg 2-3 times/day; high risk of falls and confusion
dosage_pregnancy: Avoid; causes neonatal withdrawal and cleft palate risk
side_effects: Sedation, drowsiness, impaired coordination, dependence, withdrawal syndrome, memory impairment
warnings: High dependence potential; short-term use only; taper slowly to stop; avoid alcohol; impairs driving
contraindications: Acute narrow-angle glaucoma, severe respiratory depression, myasthenia gravis, pregnancy
drug_interactions: Alcohol, opioids, other CNS depressants, ketoconazole, CYP3A4 inhibitors

uses: Anxiety, tension, and agitation. Short-term treatment of anxiety disorders.
side_effects: Sedation, drowsiness, ataxia, dependence, withdrawal
warnings: Dependence risk; short-term use; avoid alcohol and driving; do not stop abruptly
contraindications: Myasthenia gravis, sleep apnea, severe hepatic impairment, pregnancy

uses: Major depressive disorder, OCD, panic disorder, bulimia nervosa, PMDD, social anxiety disorder.
dosage_adult: 20mg once daily (morning); max 80mg/day; increase after 4 weeks if needed
dosage_elderly: 10-20mg once daily; longer half-life means slower titration
dosage_pregnancy: Category C; risks vs benefits; neonatal withdrawal possible
side_effects: Nausea, insomnia, anxiety, sexual dysfunction, weight changes, serotonin syndrome (rare), suicidal ideation in youth
warnings: Monitor for suicidal thoughts in young adults; allow 4-6 weeks for effect; long washout period; avoid MAOIs
contraindications: MAOI use within 14 days, pimozide, thioridazine
drug_interactions: MAOIs (serotonin syndrome), tramadol, triptans, lithium, warfarin, pimozide

uses: Major depression, OCD, PTSD, panic disorder, social anxiety disorder, PMDD.
dosage_adult: 50mg once daily; increase to 200mg/day if needed
dosage_child_2_12: 25mg once daily (6-12 years for OCD); increase slowly
dosage_pregnancy: Category C; neonatal adaptation syndrome possible
side_effects: Nausea, diarrhea, sexual dysfunction, insomnia, dry mouth, sweating
warnings: Monitor suicidality in young adults; 4-6 weeks for full effect; do not stop abruptly
contraindications: MAOIs within 14 days, pimozide

uses: Major depression, GAD, panic disorder, OCD, social anxiety disorder.
dosage_adult: 10mg once daily; increase to 20mg if needed
dosage_child_2_12: Not recommended (12-17 years: under specialist only)
dosage_pregnancy: Category C; PPHN risk with late pregnancy use
side_effects: Nausea, headache, insomnia, sexual dysfunction, sweating, QT prolongation
warnings: QT prolongation at higher doses; monitor in elderly; suicidality in youth warning
contraindications: MAOIs within 14 days, QT prolongation, citalopram combined
drug_interactions: MAOIs, tramadol, pimozide, QT-prolonging drugs, metoprolol

uses: Neuropathic pain (diabetic, PHN), fibromyalgia, adjunct therapy for epilepsy, GAD (off-label in Pakistan).
side_effects: Dizziness, somnolence, weight gain, blurred vision, edema, euphoria (abuse potential)
warnings: Do not stop abruptly; renal dose adjustment; abuse potential; suicidality risk; avoid driving
contraindications: Hypersensitivity

uses: Epilepsy (partial and generalized tonic-clonic), trigeminal neuralgia, bipolar disorder, neuropathic pain.
dosage_adult: 200mg twice daily initially; increase to 800-1200mg/day; therapeutic monitoring required
dosage_elderly: Start low; increased risk of SIADH and cardiac conduction effects
dosage_pregnancy: Category D; teratogenic (spina bifida); use only if essential
side_effects: Dizziness, diplopia, drowsiness, ataxia, hyponatremia, Stevens-Johnson syndrome (HLA-B*1502 carriers), agranulocytosis
warnings: Genetic testing for HLA-B*1502 in Asian patients; monitor CBC and sodium; many drug interactions
contraindications: Bone marrow suppression, MAOIs, tricyclic antidepressants, AV heart block
drug_interactions: Many CYP450 interactions — macrolides, azoles, valproate, phenytoin, warfarin, OCP

symptoms_treated: neuropathic pain,epilepsy,postherpetic neuralgia,diabetic neuropathy,restless leg syndrome
uses: Epilepsy (adjunct), postherpetic neuralgia, diabetic neuropathy, restless legs syndrome.
dosage_adult: 300mg on day 1; 300mg twice daily day 2; 300mg 3 times daily day 3; titrate to 1800-3600mg/day
side_effects: Dizziness, somnolence, ataxia, fatigue, weight gain, peripheral edema
warnings: Do not stop abruptly; renal dose adjustment; abuse potential; suicidality warning
contraindications: Hypersensitivity
drug_interactions: Antacids reduce absorption, opioids, alcohol, CNS depressants

uses: All types of epilepsy, bipolar disorder (mood stabilizer), migraine prophylaxis.
dosage_adult: 600-2400mg/day in 2-3 divided doses; serum monitoring required
dosage_child_under_2: High risk of fatal hepatotoxicity; avoid if possible
dosage_pregnancy: Category X / highest teratogenic risk; avoid absolutely if possible (neural tube, autism, cognitive defects)
side_effects: Weight gain, tremor, hair loss, GI upset, hepatotoxicity, pancreatitis, thrombocytopenia
warnings: Teratogenic — highly dangerous in pregnancy; monitor liver function and CBC; drug interactions via enzyme inhibition
contraindications: Pregnancy (unless absolutely necessary), hepatic disease, mitochondrial disease, personal/family history of hepatic porphyria

uses: Hypothyroidism, myxedema coma, thyroid cancer (as suppression therapy), non-toxic goiter.
dosage_adult: Start 25-50mcg/day; increase by 25mcg every 4-6 weeks; usual maintenance 100-200mcg/day
dosage_pregnancy: Dose often needs to increase; TSH monitoring every trimester; safe and essential in pregnancy
side_effects: Overdose: palpitations, angina, arrhythmia, tremor, weight loss, heat intolerance, insomnia
warnings: Take 30-60 min before breakfast on empty stomach; avoid with calcium/iron by 4 hours; monitor TSH 6-8 weeks after dose change
contraindications: Untreated adrenal insufficiency, uncorrected thyrotoxicosis
drug_interactions: Calcium, iron, antacids (reduce absorption), warfarin, amiodarone, phenytoin

uses: Hypothyroidism replacement therapy. Generic equivalent of Euthyrox.
side_effects: Signs of thyrotoxicosis if overdosed: palpitations, tremor, sweating
warnings: Take on empty stomach; space from interfering drugs; monitor TSH
contraindications: Thyrotoxicosis, uncorrected adrenal insufficiency

uses: Threatened and habitual miscarriage, dysmenorrhea, endometriosis, premenstrual syndrome, secondary amenorrhea, irregular menstruation.
dosage_adult: Threatened miscarriage: 40mg then 10mg every 8 hours; dysmenorrhea: 10mg twice daily days 5-25
dosage_pregnancy: First-line progestogen for threatened miscarriage; safe in pregnancy
side_effects: Nausea, headache, breast tenderness, breakthrough bleeding
warnings: Not a contraceptive; not an androgenic progestogen (better tolerated); monitor for depression
contraindications: Undiagnosed vaginal bleeding, liver tumors, sex hormone-dependent malignancy

uses: Dysfunctional uterine bleeding, dysmenorrhea, endometriosis, postponement of menstruation, contraception (mini-pill).
dosage_adult: Dysmenorrhea: 5mg 3 times/day from days 5-24; postpone menstruation: 5mg 3 times/day starting 3 days before expected period
dosage_pregnancy: Avoid in 1st trimester; may cause virilization of female fetus
side_effects: Nausea, weight gain, bloating, breakthrough bleeding, mood changes, acne
warnings: Not for use in 1st trimester; mild androgenic effect; thrombosis risk in high doses
contraindications: Pregnancy (1st trimester), thrombosis history, liver disease, undiagnosed vaginal bleeding

category: Prostaglandin E1 analog / Gastric mucosal protectant
symptoms_treated: peptic ulcer,NSAID-induced ulcer,gastric protection,cervical ripening (medical use only)
uses: Prevention and treatment of NSAID-induced gastric ulcers, gastric protection; also used in obstetrics for cervical ripening and labor induction (medical settings only).
dosage_adult: Ulcer prevention: 200mcg with each NSAID dose (4 times/day); gastric: 400-800mcg/day
dosage_pregnancy: CONTRAINDICATED for GI use (causes abortion); ONLY used under medical supervision for obstetric indications
side_effects: Diarrhea, abdominal cramps, nausea, shivering, fever
warnings: Strictly contraindicated in pregnancy for GI use; teratogenic; regulated controlled substance
contraindications: Pregnancy (for GI use), allergy to prostaglandins
substitutes: No direct OTC substitute for its GI use

symptoms_treated: abdominal cramps,IBS,stomach spasm,ureteral colic,biliary colic,intestinal spasm,menstrual cramps
uses: Abdominal, biliary, and renal colic; irritable bowel syndrome (IBS); menstrual cramps; intestinal spasm.
dosage_adult: 20mg (2 tablets) up to 4 times/day; IV/IM: 20mg every 30 minutes (max 100mg/day)
dosage_elderly: Standard dose; caution in urinary retention and glaucoma
dosage_pregnancy: Avoid in 1st trimester; used in later stages for labor under supervision
side_effects: Dry mouth, tachycardia, urinary retention, blurred vision, constipation
warnings: Avoid in glaucoma, urinary retention, myasthenia gravis; does not cross blood-brain barrier
contraindications: Glaucoma, urinary retention, myasthenia gravis, paralytic ileus

uses: Acute non-specific diarrhea, traveler's diarrhea, diarrhea-predominant IBS.
dosage_adult: 4mg initially, then 2mg after each loose stool; max 16mg/day
dosage_child_2_12: 1mg 3 times/day (2-5 years, max 3 days); 2mg 3 times/day (6-8 years); 2mg 4 times/day (9-11 years)
side_effects: Constipation, nausea, abdominal cramps, dizziness, dry mouth
warnings: Do not use in infectious diarrhea with fever/blood; avoid in children under 2; stop if no improvement in 48 hours
contraindications: Infectious diarrhea (fever/blood), children under 2, acute ulcerative colitis, C. difficile
drug_interactions: Saquinavir, ritonavir, quinidine (increase loperamide plasma levels)

uses: Constipation, bowel preparation before procedures, post-operative constipation.
dosage_child_2_12: 5mg tablet at night (4+ years); suppository 5mg (under 10 years)
dosage_pregnancy: Avoid; stimulant laxatives not preferred; use bulk-forming agents
side_effects: Abdominal cramps, diarrhea, hypokalemia (prolonged use), nausea
warnings: Do not take within 1 hour of antacids or milk; not for long-term use; may cause electrolyte imbalance
contraindications: Intestinal obstruction, acute abdominal conditions, inflammatory bowel disease
drug_interactions: Diuretics, antacids, milk (reduce enteric coating effectiveness)

formula: NaCl + KCl + Na citrate + Glucose (WHO low-osmolarity formula)
uses: Prevention and treatment of dehydration due to diarrhea, vomiting, cholera, heat exhaustion.
dosage_adult: 200-400ml after each loose stool; total 2-4 liters/day as needed
dosage_child_2_12: 50-100ml/kg over 2-4 hours for mild-moderate dehydration; 200ml after each loose stool
dosage_child_under_2: 50-100ml/kg over 4 hours (WHO protocol); give with spoon or syringe
dosage_elderly: Encourage as tolerated; monitor for hypernatremia
side_effects: Nausea (if taken too fast), hypernatremia (if ORS made incorrectly)
warnings: Prepare correctly with safe water; do not add sugar; seek medical help for severe dehydration
contraindications: Severe dehydration (requires IV), ileus
available_forms: Sachets (dissolve in 1 liter water); Ready-to-drink solution

uses: Rheumatoid arthritis, osteoarthritis, pain relief, dysmenorrhea.
side_effects: GI upset, nausea, headache, dizziness, edema
warnings: Take with food; GI risk; avoid in peptic ulcer; cardiovascular risk
contraindications: Active peptic ulcer, severe renal/hepatic impairment, 3rd trimester pregnancy
